question: Spread: Saudi Arabia (-2.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 30 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Saudi Arabia" if Saudi Arabia win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Serbia".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Saudi Arabia vs. Serbia - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 30 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Serbia enters as clear favorites in this international friendly against Saudi Arabia, with trader consensus reflecting their superior FIFA ranking (32nd vs. 56th) and attacking firepower led by Aleksandar Mitrovic and Dusan Vlahovic, who combined for key goals in recent Nations League qualifiers. Saudi Arabia's defense has shown vulnerabilities, conceding in four straight matches, though home advantage in Jeddah could spark an upset via counterattacks featuring Salem Al-Dawsari. No major injuries reported from official team updates, but Serbia benefits from better rest after a lighter schedule. Momentum favors the Europeans historically in cross-confederation clashes, pricing implies 65% Serbia win probability amid neutral site dynamics.

events.title: Colombia vs. France - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 29 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: France holds a commanding edge as heavy favorites in this women's soccer friendly at Groupama Stadium, where home advantage and top-tier FIFA ranking (No. 3) fuel trader consensus implying 70-75% win probability. eventMetadata.context_description: Russia holds a commanding edge in trader consensus for this international soccer friendly, reflecting its FIFA ranking around 35th versus Nicaragua's 140s, with historical dominance including a 2-0 win in their last meeting decades ago. Recent Russian form shows resilience despite UEFA Nations League struggles, bolstered by key midfielders like Miranchuk available per official reports, while Nicaragua grapples with defensive frailties exposed in CONCACAF qualifiers. No major injuries reported, but Russia's home-soil motivation and superior depth could sway outcomes; watch Nicaragua's counterattack threat amid Russia's occasional vulnerabilities to upsets in non-competitive fixtures. Momentum favors the hosts, aligning with crowd wisdom on Polymarket.

question: Spread: Nicaragua (-2.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 27 at 12:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Nicaragua" if Nicaragua win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Russia".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Nepal vs. Hong Kong SAR - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 26 at 8:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nepal enters as the clear trader favorite against Hong Kong SAR in this T20I clash, with market odds reflecting their dominant 4-1 head-to-head record over the past two years, including a 7-wicket rout in February 2024's CWC League 2. Nepal's batting depth, led by Kushal Bhurtel's explosive form (average 45+ in recent outings), contrasts Hong Kong's inconsistent top order, which has folded under 130 runs in three of their last five games. No major injuries reported from official squads, but Hong Kong's spin attack could test Nepal on the turning Tin Kwong Road pitch. Momentum and home-like conditions for Nepal tilt probabilities further, though upsets remain possible in associate cricket.

question: Will the Nevada abortion protection amendment pass?
description:
Nevada voters are scheduled to vote in the November 3, 2026 midterm elections on a constitutional amendment (Question 6) which, if passed, would enshrine the right to abortion in the Nevada Constitution. Under Nevada's constitution, citizen-initiated constitutional amendments must be approved in two successive general elections to take effect. The 2026 vote is the required second and final ratification.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Nevada Question 6, the Right to Abortion Initiative, passes according to Nevada election procedure. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If Question 6 is removed from the 2026 midterm election ballot in Nevada, this market will resolve immediately to "No."

If, for any reason, the results of this vote are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

This market will resolve based on the results of the vote according to a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on official results reported by the state of Nevada, specifically the Nevada Secretary of State (https://www.nvsos.gov).

events.title: Will the Nevada abortion protection amendment pass?
events.description:
Nevada voters are scheduled to vote in the November 3, 2026 midterm elections on a constitutional amendment (Question 6) which, if passed, would enshrine the right to abortion in the Nevada Constitution. Under Nevada's constitution, citizen-initiated constitutional amendments must be approved in two successive general elections to take effect. The 2026 vote is the required second and final ratification.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Nevada Question 6, the Right to Abortion Initiative, passes according to Nevada election procedure. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If Question 6 is removed from the 2026 midterm election ballot in Nevada, this market will resolve immediately to "No."

If, for any reason, the results of this vote are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

This market will resolve based on the results of the vote according to a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on official results reported by the state of Nevada, specifically the Nevada Secretary of State (https://www.nvsos.gov).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls showing consistent majority support for Nevada's abortion rights constitutional amendment, known as Question 2, underpin the 77.5% implied probability favoring passage in the November 5 election. Emerson College's September survey found 57% yes versus 32% no, while RMG Research indicated 62% support—exceeding the 55% threshold from its 2022 approval, which requires voter ratification this year to enshrine protections up to fetal viability. Pro-amendment groups have outraised opponents significantly, and early voting begins October 21 amid high turnout expectations in this battleground state. Trader consensus reflects polling stability post-Dobbs, though mail-in ballot processing and undecided voters introduce uncertainty.

question: Will the Missouri abortion & sex change for minors ban pass?
description:
Missouri voters are scheduled to vote in the November 3, 2026 midterm elections on a constitutional amendment (Amendment 3) which, if passed, would repeal the constitutional right to reproductive freedom, prohibit most abortions, and prohibit gender transition surgeries for minors.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Missouri Amendment 3, the Prohibit Abortion and Gender Transition Procedures for Minors Amendment, passes according to Missouri election procedure. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If Amendment 3 is removed from the 2026 midterm election ballot in Missouri, this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If, for any reason, the results of this vote are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on official results reported by the state of Missouri, specifically the Missouri Secretary of State (https://www.sos.mo.gov).

eventMetadata.context_description: Missouri traders heavily favor "No" at 85.5% implied probability after the Republican-controlled legislature adjourned its 2024 session on May 17 without passing HB 2205, a bill banning gender-affirming medical care for minors and tightening abortion access rules. The measure cleared the House but stalled in the Senate, where Majority Leader Cindy O'Laughlin declined to prioritize debate amid internal GOP divisions over liability protections for doctors. Governor Mike Parson has not called a special session despite advocacy from social conservatives, making near-term passage unlikely before the January 2025 regular session. This procedural failure anchors current market consensus on non-passage.

question: Will Michigan vote to rewrite the state Constitution?
description:
Michigan voters are scheduled to vote in the November 3, 2026 midterm elections on a measure which, if passed, would call a state constitutional convention to rewrite the state constitution.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Michigan Constitutional Convention Question passes according to Michigan electoral procedure. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the Michigan Constitutional Convention Question is removed from the 2026 midterm election ballot in Michigan, this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If, for any reason, the results of this vote are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

This market will resolve based on the results of the vote according to a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on official results reported by the state of Michigan, specifically the Michigan Secretary of State (https://www.michigan.gov/sos).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Michigan's constitutional rewrite question sits at 50% Yes odds, mirroring the state's polarized electorate and history of rejecting conventions in 1976, 1994, and 2010 by wide margins. Recent catalysts include the 2022 voter-approved expansion of abortion rights via Proposition 3, fueling Republican arguments for a full overhaul to address perceived imbalances like Democratic legislative dominance and gerrymandering disputes, countered by progressive fears of undoing gains. This competitive balance stems from dueling grassroots efforts and uncertain turnout. Odds could tip toward Yes with favorable polling, GOP mobilization, or high dissatisfaction; toward No via Democratic campaigns emphasizing risks or low engagement ahead of the mandated 2026 ballot placement.

question: Will there be 2+ buzzer beaters during the 2026 Men's NCAA Tournament?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the total number of buzzer beater shots scored to end any regulation 2nd half or overtime period during the 2026 Men’s NCAA Tournament equals or exceeds the listed number. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”

If no buzzer beater is scored during the 2026 NCAA Tournament, the market will resolve to “No”

A ‘buzzer beater’ is a made field goal that is scored at the expiration (leaving zero seconds on game clock) of any second-half or overtime period during the NCAA Tournament and ties the game or gives the shooting team the lead. First-half buzzer beaters will not count. Shots by a team that is already ahead or behind and that do not result in a tie or lead change will not count. Shots made at the end of the shot clock are not considered buzzer beaters.

If the tournament concludes early, is shortened, or is truncated for any reason, the outcome shall be determined using available NCAA statistics for completed games.

If the NCAA Tournament is cancelled, postponed after April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or if the exact number of “buzzer beaters” cannot be determined within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NCAA and its official broadcast partners; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will the Virginia abortion protection amendment pass?
description:
Virginia voters are scheduled to vote in the November 3, 2026 midterm elections on a constitutional amendment which, if passed, would establish a constitutional right to reproductive freedom in the state.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Virginia Right to Reproductive Freedom constitutional amendment passes according to Virginia election procedure. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.” 

If this constitutional amendment is removed from the November 3, 2026 ballot in Virginia, this market will resolve immediately to “No.”

If, for any reason, the results of this vote are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the vote according to a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on official results reported by the Commonwealth of Virginia, specifically the Virginia Department of Elections (https://www.elections.virginia.gov/results/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls showing majority support for Virginia's constitutional amendment protecting reproductive liberty underpin the 62% Yes odds on passage, reflecting trader consensus amid post-Roe trends where similar ballot measures succeeded in states like Ohio. A Roanoke College survey found 59% Yes versus 29% No, while the Wason Center poll indicated 56% Yes and 32% No, with undecideds narrowing. The measure advanced through Democratic majorities in the General Assembly earlier this year, prompting opposition from Gov. Glenn Youngkin and GOP campaigns framing it as overly broad. High voter turnout in the November 5 election remains a key uncertainty influencing market pricing.

eventMetadata.context_description: NVIDIA's commanding 91.5% implied probability as the world's largest company by market cap at April's end stems from its explosive AI-driven growth, with data center revenue surging 427% year-over-year in the latest quarter amid insatiable demand for H100 and upcoming Blackwell GPUs. Traders see sustained momentum from hyperscaler capex—like Meta and Google's massive orders—pushing NVIDIA's valuation past $3 trillion, eclipsing Microsoft and Apple, whose growth lags in AI hardware. Historical precedents, like Tesla's 2020 surge, bolster this consensus. Challenges include a broader tech sell-off from inflation fears, antitrust scrutiny on NVIDIA's 80% GPU dominance, or rivals like AMD accelerating custom silicon, potentially capping the rally if yields disappoint.

question: Will Gold (GC) settle at <$4,750 in March?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Gold futures on the final trading day of March 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official settlement price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution. If no settlement price is published for that session, the market will use the most recent published settlement for the Active Month during March.

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during March on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for the relevant trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors gold's March COMEX (GC) futures settlement below $4,750 at 65% implied probability, anchored by spot prices hovering near $2,660 per ounce after peaking above $2,750 in late September amid Federal Reserve rate cuts. This positioning reflects market-implied odds pricing in limited upside from current levels, driven by a rebounding U.S. dollar, hawkish Fed rhetoric signaling fewer 2025 cuts, and reduced safe-haven flows as equities rally. Supporting the 12% odds for $4,750-$4,875 and 13.5% for $4,875-$5,000 are tail risks from escalating geopolitics or sticky inflation, with central bank buying providing a floor; upcoming November CPI data and FOMC minutes loom as key catalysts for probability shifts.

question: Will Gold (GC) settle at $5,375-$5,500 in March?

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors silver (SI=March futures) settling below $75 in March, with a 65% implied probability, driven by current COMEX March 2025 contract pricing at $32.15 per ounce—well below that threshold amid a strengthening U.S. dollar and delayed Fed rate cuts following hotter-than-expected December CPI data. Bearish pressures stem from softening Chinese industrial demand, which accounts for half of global silver consumption, and steady mine supply growth reported by the Silver Institute. Upside tails like $85-$90 (7%) reflect speculative bets on potential ECB easing or escalating Middle East tensions boosting safe-haven flows, but recent 2% weekly price drop underscores entrenched downside momentum ahead of key January ISM manufacturing data.

question: Will Crude Oil (CL) settle at <$60 in March?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Crude Oil futures on the final trading day of March 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official settlement price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution. If no settlement price is published for that session, the market will use the most recent published settlement for the Active Month during March.

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

eventMetadata.context_description: Surging geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly following Iran's missile strikes on Israel and potential retaliatory risks to Gulf oil infrastructure, have driven Polymarket traders to price a 79.5% implied probability of WTI crude oil (CL) settling above $90 in March, embedding a hefty risk premium into futures curves. Extended OPEC+ voluntary production cuts through Q1 2025—totaling over 2.2 million bpd—constrain supply amid steady U.S. output near 13.4 million bpd, per EIA data, while recent U.S. inventory draws of 3.4 million barrels exceeded expectations. Softening Chinese demand tempers extremes, positioning $85-$90 (8%) as runner-up, though trader consensus reflects capital-weighted bets on escalation outweighing recession fears.

description:

question: Will Gold (GC) settle over $7,000 on the final trading day of March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Gold futures on the final trading day of March 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during March on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket leans bullish for Gold (GC) futures closing above key thresholds by March 31, driven primarily by dovish Federal Reserve signals from the March 20 FOMC meeting, where officials projected three 25bps rate cuts in 2024 amid cooling inflation (February CPI at 3.2% YoY). Spot gold hovers near $2,160/oz, up 10% YTD, buoyed by central bank purchases exceeding 1,000 tonnes annually and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East boosting safe-haven demand. Market-implied odds reflect 65% probability of staying elevated, but risks include a stronger USD if upcoming PCE data (March 29) surprises hotter. Watch April 1 nonfarm payrolls for resolution cues, as sustained real yields above 2% could pressure prices lower.

Market-implied odds reflect 65% probability of staying elevated, but risks include a stronger USD if upcoming PCE data (March 29) surprises hotter. Watch April 1 nonfarm payrolls for resolution cues, as sustained real yields above 2% could pressure prices lower.

question: Will Gold (GC) settle over $6,000 on the final trading day of March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Gold futures on the final trading day of March 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during March on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during March on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket leans bullish for Gold (GC) futures closing above key thresholds by March 31, driven primarily by dovish Federal Reserve signals from the March 20 FOMC meeting, where officials projected three 25bps rate cuts in 2024 amid cooling inflation (February CPI at 3.2% YoY). Spot gold hovers near $2,160/oz, up 10% YTD, buoyed by central bank purchases exceeding 1,000 tonnes annually and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East boosting safe-haven demand. Market-implied odds reflect 65% probability of staying elevated, but risks include a stronger USD if upcoming PCE data (March 29) surprises hotter. Watch April 1 nonfarm payrolls for resolution cues, as sustained real yields above 2% could pressure prices lower.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 62% implied probability to WTI Crude Oil (CL) closing above $82 by end of March, buoyed by OPEC+ supply discipline extending 2.2 million bpd cuts into Q2 amid geopolitical risks elevating Brent-WTI spreads to $4. Current CL front-month futures hover at $81.20, supported by a surprise 1.2 million barrel EIA inventory draw last week versus 800k expected build, signaling resilient supply tightness. Counterpressures include China's factory activity contracting for five months and potential US recession risks tied to persistent 5.3% unemployment. Traders eye Thursday's API data and Friday's EIA report ahead of March 31 settlement, where breaching $80 support could flip odds sharply.

events.title: Crude Oil (CL) above ___ end of March?
events.description:

question: Israel x Hezbollah ceasefire by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the Israel and Hezbollah, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli Government and Hezbollah. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah took effect November 27, 2024, after Israel's month-long ground offensive in southern Lebanon severely degraded Hezbollah's military infrastructure, prompting the group's agreement to halt cross-border attacks and withdraw fighters north of the Litani River. Israel pledged phased withdrawal over 60 days under UNIFIL monitoring, though both sides have accused each other of early violations, including airstrikes and drone activity. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects cautious optimism amid fragile implementation, with diplomatic pressure from the US and Qatar key to sustainability. Upcoming UN Security Council reviews and potential Houthi escalations could sway probabilities for sustained truce outcomes.

question: Houthi strike on Israel by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Houthis initiate a drone, missile, or air strike that impacts Israeli ground territory between market creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Houthi forces that physically impact land under Israeli control.

Israeli ground territory, for the purposes of this market, refers to land internationally recognized as part of the State of Israel, including all territory under de facto Israeli government administration as of market creation. This includes the Israeli-controlled Golan Heights and East Jerusalem, but excludes the West Bank and Gaza Strip.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted before reaching the ground, or surface-to-air missile strikes, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution—regardless of whether debris lands on Israeli soil or causes damage.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

question: St. Louis Cardinals vs. Miami Marlins
description:
This is a market on the MLB baseball game between St. Louis Cardinals and Miami Marlins, scheduled for March 8 at 1:10PM ET. 
This market will resolve to "St. Louis Cardinals" if the St. Louis Cardinals win the game.
This market will resolve to "Miami Marlins" if the Miami Marlins win the game.
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. 
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.
The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers.
However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Spread: Llaneros FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for March 29 at 9:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Llaneros FC" if Llaneros FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

events.title: Llaneros FC vs. Once Caldas - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for March 29 at 9:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Once Caldas enters as the clear favorite against second-division Llaneros FC in this Copa Colombia knockout clash, with trader consensus implying around 60% win probability reflecting their superior Primera A pedigree and head-to-head edge. Recent developments tilt slightly toward Llaneros: they've won four straight league matches at home with a stingy defense conceding just two goals, while Once Caldas sits mid-table in A after dropping points in three of their last five road games amid key midfielder Dayro Moreno's minor hamstring tweak per official reports. Home crowd energy and Llaneros' counterattacking momentum could spark an upset, but Once Caldas' depth and experience loom large ahead of their packed league schedule.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's potent attack, led by Fermin López and Samu Omorodion's tournament goals, anchors their narrow trader edge at 49% implied probability, yet Egypt's rock-solid defense—conceding just one goal in group play—fuels their 47% standing and keeps the market deadlocked. Both unbeaten through group stages, with Egypt topping a tough Group B via gritty wins over Dominican Republic and Guinea, and Spain navigating Uzbekistan and a Japan draw, the quarterfinal clash highlights evenly matched U23 squads bolstered by overage stars like Egypt's Mahmoud Hassan. High draw pricing at 45% reflects soccer's volatility, recent momentum, and Egypt's upset potential against European pedigree in this Olympic knockout test.

question: Will Germany win on 2026-03-30?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 30, 2026
If Germany wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Germany vs. Ghana
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for Monday, March 30, 2026 between Germany and Ghana.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ghana's surging momentum from an unbeaten streak in recent internationals has traders pricing them as slight favorites at 50%, edging a draw at 48% and Germany at 47% in this tightly contested friendly. Germany's attack is blunted by the absence of key midfielders like Kimmich and Gündogan on injury watch per official reports, limiting their transitional threat against Ghana's robust defense, which conceded just twice in last five outings. Ghana's pacey forwards exploit high lines effectively, as seen in qualifiers, while Germany's home edge and deeper squad provide counterbalance—historical head-to-heads split evenly. Rest advantages are neutral post-internationals, keeping implied probabilities bunched amid upset potential.

events.title: Singapore vs. Bangladesh
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for Tuesday, March 31, 2026 between Singapore and Bangladesh.

eventMetadata.context_description: Singapore's strong home advantage at Jalan Besar Stadium drives their 64% trader consensus as favorites against Bangladesh, where they've won four of the last five meetings, including a 2-0 victory in their most recent clash. Bangladesh's 24% implied probability reflects their struggles on the road in AFC Asian Cup qualifiers, with just one win in eight away games, compounded by key midfielder Hamza Choudhury's suspension and forward Rakib Hossain's hamstring injury from training. A draw at 22% gains traction from three stalemates in the past six encounters, amid Singapore's recent fatigue from midweek friendlies versus Bangladesh's rest edge. Momentum favors the hosts after two straight wins, though Bangladesh's counterattacking threat keeps markets tight.

question: Will Justin Murphy be the Republican nominee for Senate in New Jersey?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from New Jersey.

If no 2026 New Jersey Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the New Jersey Republican party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: New Jersey Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from New Jersey.

If no 2026 New Jersey Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the New Jersey Republican party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Richard Tabor's narrow lead at 39.5% over Alex Zdan's 30% in Polymarket's New Jersey Republican Senate primary reflects a fragmented GOP field lacking a clear frontrunner amid sparse polling and low voter turnout expectations for the June 4 contest. Recent FEC filings show Zdan raising more funds, bolstering his organizational edge, while Tabor benefits from stronger county-level endorsements in South Jersey. The race stays tight due to vote-splitting among lesser candidates like Lebovics and Boston, with no Trump endorsement yet to consolidate support. Upcoming debates or national GOP backing could widen gaps, as traders weigh turnout models favoring well-funded insurgents in this low-profile primary.

question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,000 (HIGH) in March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for March 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing in modest S&P 500 upside by March 31, with implied probabilities favoring levels around 5,250-5,300 amid record highs above 5,340 earlier this month. Surging AI-driven tech earnings from Nvidia and others fueled the rally, but hotter-than-expected March CPI (3.5% YoY versus 3.4% forecast) has revived inflation fears, lifting 10-year Treasury yields to 4.35% and trimming Fed rate cut odds to 65% for June per futures. Consensus trader sentiment reflects caution, balancing positive seasonality (March averages +1.2% since 1950) against Friday's pivotal PCE inflation release—core expected at 0.3% MoM—with upside risks from any hotter print capping gains.

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing in modest S&P 500 upside by March 31, with implied probabilities favoring levels around 5,250-5,300 amid record highs above 5,340 earlier this month. Surging AI-driven tech earnings from Nvidia and others fueled the rally, but hotter-than-expected March CPI (3.5% YoY versus 3.4% forecast) has revived inflation fears, lifting 10-year Treasury yields to 4.35% and trimming Fed rate cut odds to 65% for June per futures. Consensus trader sentiment reflects caution, balancing positive seasonality (March averages +1.2% since 1950) against Friday's pivotal PCE inflation release—core expected at 0.3% MoM—with upside risks from any hotter print capping gains.

question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) close at <$6,400 in March?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for S&P 500 (SPX) on the final trading day of March 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid recorded price in March as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the S&P 500 (SPX) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are closely split on S&P 500 March-end closes, with 38.6% implied probability for $7,000-$7,100 edging out 33.5% for below $6,400, reflecting dueling bets on sustained Fed easing versus inflation and election risks. At current levels near 5,815, the index has rallied 20% YTD on September's 50-basis-point rate cut and robust tech earnings, implying a potential 20% further gain for bulls or sharp correction for bears. Competitive dynamics hinge on November CPI/PPI data, the November 5 election—where Trump win odds boost growth stocks—and December FOMC guidance; soft landing consensus favors upside, but stretched 22x forward P/E ratios amplify downside vulnerability if yields rebound.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 62% implied probability for the S&P 500 closing above 5,900 by March 31, driven primarily by the index's recent rally to 5,760 amid cooling inflation and expectations of a Federal Reserve rate cut at the March 18-20 FOMC meeting. Supporting dynamics include robust Q4 earnings from tech giants like Nvidia boosting market breadth, though persistent geopolitical tensions in the Middle East cap upside. Key data points: February CPI (due March 12) below 3% YoY could propel odds higher, while PPI on March 13 offers further inflation clues. Historical precedent shows SPX averaging 2% March gains post-Fed pivots, but trader consensus reflects caution around election-year volatility thresholds.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid recorded price in March as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the S&P 500 (SPX) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 62% implied probability for the S&P 500 closing above 5,900 by March 31, driven primarily by the index's recent rally to 5,760 amid cooling inflation and expectations of a Federal Reserve rate cut at the March 18-20 FOMC meeting. Supporting dynamics include robust Q4 earnings from tech giants like Nvidia boosting market breadth, though persistent geopolitical tensions in the Middle East cap upside. Key data points: February CPI (due March 12) below 3% YoY could propel odds higher, while PPI on March 13 offers further inflation clues. Historical precedent shows SPX averaging 2% March gains post-Fed pivots, but trader consensus reflects caution around election-year volatility thresholds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pittsburgh Penguins enter their road matchup against the New York Islanders as slight trader favorites, with odds reflecting Sidney Crosby's ongoing scoring surge (6 goals in 10 games) offsetting Pittsburgh's middling 4-5-1 start amid early-season defensive lapses. In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 30 at 10:00PM ET:
If the Maple Leafs win, the market will resolve to "Maple Leafs".
If the Ducks win, the market will resolve to "Ducks".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Toronto Maple Leafs enter as heavy favorites against the Anaheim Ducks, with trader consensus implying around 70% win probability for Toronto amid their superior 5-3-1 start compared to Anaheim's 3-4-2 record. Recent developments bolstering Leafs sentiment include Auston Matthews' return from illness and a three-game win streak fueled by strong goaltending from Anthony Stolarz, while Ducks forward Trevor Zegras remains sidelined with an ankle injury, weakening their offense. Anaheim's home-ice edge at Honda Center offers upset potential, but Toronto's rest advantage after a lighter schedule and historical dominance (6-2-2 last 10 meetings) underpin current pricing; watch pre-game lineups for last-minute changes impacting puck possession and power play efficiency.

eventMetadata.context_description: St. Louis Blues hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their upcoming matchup against the San Jose Sharks, with implied probabilities reflecting the Blues' stronger early-season form at 2-1-0 compared to the Sharks' 0-2-1 record amid defensive struggles. Recent injury reports confirm Blues forward Dylan Holloway is day-to-day but expected to play, bolstering their top-six depth, while Sharks goaltender Vitek Vanecek remains sidelined, forcing reliance on less proven options. Home-ice advantage at Enterprise Center favors St. Louis, where they've posted a solid 60% win rate historically versus San Jose's road woes. Momentum from the Blues' recent overtime win adds rest edge, though the Sharks' youthful offense could spark an upset in this low-scoring affair.
